title: Everything you need to know about Zach Bryan’s planned Jack Kerouac Center in Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Zach Bryan purchased the St. Jean Baptiste Church in Lowell to create the Jack Kerouac Center.
The center will feature a museum, performance venue, recording studio, and restaurant, honoring Kerouac's legacy.
Renovation costs for the ADA-compliant center could reach $30 million, with fundraising efforts underway.

title: Brookline runner takes on first Boston Marathon for One World Strong
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Elena Castellini, 23, from Brookline, will run her first Boston Marathon to support the One World Strong Foundation.
The One World Strong Foundation, led by 2013 bombing survivor Dave Fortier, aids trauma victims globally, including in Ukraine.
Castellini moved to Boston from Monaco in 2019 and is using the marathon to honor her new home city.

title: South Boston woman fatally stabbed boyfriend during an argument, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, was charged with manslaughter for stabbing her boyfriend, Kyle Bradford, during an argument in South Boston.
Pascual pleaded not guilty, claiming self-defense, and is being held on a $100,000 bond after her arraignment.
Bradford arrived drunk and assaulted Pascual before she stabbed him; he had previous charges, including domestic assault.

title: Patriots to be featured on HBO’s ‘Hard Knocks’ ahead of 2027 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots will be featured on HBO's 'Hard Knocks' for the 2027 NFL season.
Seattle Seahawks will be showcased on 'Hard Knocks' for the 2026 season, following their Super Bowl win.
Recent NFL rule changes increased playoff teams' eligibility for 'Hard Knocks', allowing the Patriots' inclusion.

title: James Hagens notches first points with Providence Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens scored his first professional goal in Providence Bruins' 6-4 win against the Bridgeport Islanders.
Hagens recorded a primary assist on a goal during Providence's 4-2 loss to the Springfield Thunderbirds.
Hagens, playing on an AHL amateur tryout, could potentially be promoted to the Boston Bruins this season.

title: Could Patriots circle back with Stefon Diggs? Here’s what Eliot Wolf said of a potential reunion.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots released Stefon Diggs due to a $26.8 million cap hit, opting for younger wideout Romeo Doubs instead.
Executive Eliot Wolf stated the door isn't closed on re-signing Diggs, mentioning potential scenarios like injuries or scheme changes.
Diggs recorded 1,013 receiving yards in 2025 but struggled in the postseason, casting uncertainty on his WR1 role at age 32.

title: Man sentenced for raping 2 women in Framingham store in 1989
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Stephen Paul Gale, 73, sentenced to at least 50 years for 1989 Framingham store rapes.
Gale convicted on charges including aggravated rape, kidnapping, and armed robbery in Middlesex Superior Court.
Cold case solved using DNA genealogy; victims delivered impact statements at sentencing.

title: Celtics’ Joe Mazzulla on possibility of winning Coach of the Year: ‘It’s a stupid award’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla criticizes the NBA Coach of the Year award, calling it 'stupid' and unnecessary.
Mazzulla emphasizes the importance of staff and players, attributing team success to their efforts.
The Celtics achieved their fifth consecutive 50-win season, holding the second spot in the Eastern Conference.

title: 1 dead after crash in Mattapan, police say
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: One person died in a car crash in Mattapan late Sunday night, according to Boston police.
The incident occurred at 11:14 p.m. at the intersection of American Legion Highway and Walk Hill Street.
Police found the victim inside the heavily damaged car, which was propped on the sidewalk.

title: Should Joe Mazzulla win Coach of the Year? Here’s how he stacks up against the competition.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Joe Mazzulla has the second-best odds for NBA Coach of the Year, behind Detroit's J.B. Bickerstaff.
The Celtics, under Mazzulla, have the second-best record in the Eastern Conference despite significant roster changes.
J.B. Bickerstaff led the Detroit Pistons to the top seed in the East after a last-place finish two seasons ago.
